title: How does Shinji dress
wikitext:
[]: How does Shinji dress?             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Aug 23, 2008 8:37 pm Why is he seen in his uniform so often? What else does he wear? I think I remember some XTC shirt. That's all I remember. Oh, and also, what would you think he would wear? What would influence him?

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Sat Aug 23, 2008 9:09 pm Doesn't he wear a pink T-shirt in EoE when he's strangling his beloved Asuka? Well, Rei is always (at least I think so) either in her school uniform or plug suit. Then again, she's socially abnormal. Anyways, if Shinji's school uniform looked like that, I wouldn't mind wearing it as 'street clothes.' It's black pants + nice shirt. Normal enough, unlike the girls' uniforms.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 23, 2008 9:18 pm Asuka changes into things besides her uniform when she gets out of school Rei just does what she's told, so she really doesn't own other clothes besides functional school uniforms (and like, a bathing suit or two, again, purely functional) Toji and Kensuke don't really wear other outfits that much either I think its just that as "guys", they don't really have a great need to wear other outfits much of the time Shinji does, when in "lounging about the house" mode, wear a few loose t-shirts/shorts, but that's "around the house" stuff not "stuff I'd wear with other people around" Btw, what does that logo on the T-Shirt Shinji wears (i.e. in episode 8) mean?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 23, 2008 9:25 pm ADV translated the Kanji as "Daily Calm Spirit" but some online site that sells the shirt translated it to be a phrase of sorts, one I can't remember at the moment. I'll post in here again when I find it. Oh and you're thinking of episode 9, V. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Aug 24, 2008 2:00 am Doesn't he wear a pink T-shirt in EoE when he's strangling his beloved Asuka?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Aug 24, 2008 5:09 am How does Shinji dress? By putting his trousers on one leg at a time what would you think he would wear?

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Aug 24, 2008 1:49 pm It might be a symbol of his general attitude to just do as he's told. Uniforms are mandated by the schools, aren't they?

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Aug 24, 2008 3:07 pm In Japan, yes. I think in Korea also but that's offtopic. You can ask Imp for confirmation.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 24, 2008 3:13 pm Well, I will just confirm now, uniforms are for: Private Schools and for State Schools. However, Municipal-run schools are the only schools (with a few private schools) that do not have uniforms. With Shinji only wearing his uniform, I think it might be because he is lacking in individual initiative...

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sun Aug 24, 2008 4:13 pm Wow, I forgot all about the pink shirt. The Imperialist wrote: With Shinji only wearing his uniform, I think it might be because he is lacking in individual initiative...

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Sun Aug 24, 2008 7:58 pm Isn't what Toji wears his school athletic clothes? Maybe the teacher doesn't care.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 24, 2008 8:51 pm In a scene in episode 9 Touji actually wears his school uniform. But the fact he usually wears his athletic clothes means either the school is lax about the uniform policy or everybody's too scared of him to make him wear otherwise. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Aug 24, 2008 9:22 pm [ he dresses to kill hes cute

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Aug 24, 2008 9:23 pm yeah the theory on Toji is that either 1- discipline is so lax at the school that no one (i.e. the old senile professor) even tries to stop him, or 2 - ...I've heard that in Japan they make misbehaving students wear a different uniform, sort of like a dunce cap. Aaron mentioned this once. Nonetheless, I always thought #1. Aren't school uniforms mandatory? Well if that's the case, I thought it highlighted the fact that discipline is SO lax in Class 2-A's school (kids could actually run around the room looking out windows and no one even tries to stop them, etc. Hikari tries to promote order on her own but its really from the ground-up)

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Aug 24, 2008 9:23 pm If you find something that works for you, why change?

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Mon Aug 25, 2008 1:09 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: ADV translated the Kanji as "Daily Calm Spirit" but some online site that sells the shirt translated it to be a phrase of sorts, one I can't remember at the moment. I'll post in here again when I find it. Oh and you're thinking of episode 9, V. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelion__x » Mon Aug 25, 2008 2:10 am He wears Converse sneakers, apparently.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Aug 25, 2008 2:56 am In Japan, the only lax schools are the municipal run schools (Kouritsu). However, in a State School (Kokuritsu) because they are in the end, trying to develop elite beaureaucrats, they are not so lax, and in private schools, they are trying to develop the elites of companies and whatnot, they are not so lax either. THe only reason Kouritsu schools are lax is because they are afraid of the parents of the students, who think that schools are a 'service' because they pay for the municipal school, as they get their funds from the area they are in while in State schools, they have the backing of the State, not the people in the area so they can basically shrug off most complaints, and in private schools, it is basically "If you don't like it, you can go because many people want to be in our school" attitude, so they can shrug off most ridiculous complaints.* Toji's athletic clothes, is not actually uncommmon. However, it is only really worn by established athletes who have to constantly go to extra-curricular activities, so they are allowed to wear it. *In many schools, they still make you stand on the corridors holding buckets of water

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Mon Aug 25, 2008 3:55 am The Imperialist wrote: In Japan, the only lax schools are the municipal run schools (Kouritsu). However, in a State School (Kokuritsu) because they are in the end, trying to develop elite beaureaucrats, they are not so lax, and in private schools, they are trying to develop the elites of companies and whatnot, they are not so lax either. THe only reason Kouritsu schools are lax is because they are afraid of the parents of the students, who think that schools are a 'service' because they pay for the municipal school, as they get their funds from the area they are in while in State schools, they have the backing of the State, not the people in the area so they can basically shrug off most complaints, and in private schools, it is basically "If you don't like it, you can go because many people want to be in our school" attitude, so they can shrug off most ridiculous complaints.*

[]:             Quote Post by p06 » Mon Aug 25, 2008 4:15 am By "athletic clothes" do you mean tracksuit? Toji's athletic clothes, is not actually uncommmon. However, it is only really worn by established athletes who have to constantly go to extra-curricular activities, so they are allowed to wear it.
